description: Enhanced Victorian NDIS red flag categories, keywords, and required forms for 2026 compliance

red_flag_categories.name: Unauthorised Restrictive Practice
red_flag_categories.description: Physical guidance, restraint, seclusion, or restricting movement, communication, or access without documented authorisation in a Behaviour Support Plan.
red_flag_categories.context_sensitivity_note: This category contains medication names (antipsychotics, benzodiazepines, antidepressants, etc.) which are valid indicators of potential chemical restraint but may cause false positives when medications are mentioned in routine clinical contexts (e.g., 'administered prescribed Seroquel at 8pm as per medication chart'). The safety scan logic should consider context: medication names alone in the presence of routine administration language (e.g., 'as prescribed', 'medication chart', 'regular dose') may warrant a lower confidence flag rather than an automatic red.

red_flag_categories.name: Medication Error / Missed Medication
red_flag_categories.description: Any medication administration error including wrong dose, wrong medication, wrong time, missed dose, or administration without proper authorisation.

red_flag_categories.name: Injury / Fall / Medical Emergency
red_flag_categories.description: Any physical harm, fall, medical event, or emergency requiring medical attention or hospital visit.

red_flag_categories.name: Abuse / Neglect / Exploitation Indicators
red_flag_categories.description: Any indicators of verbal, physical, sexual, or emotional abuse, neglect, or financial exploitation of the participant.

red_flag_categories.name: Missing Consent / Capacity Concerns
red_flag_categories.description: Decisions made without proper consent, or concerns about a participant's decision-making capacity.

red_flag_categories.name: Property Damage / Financial Irregularity
red_flag_categories.description: Damage to participant's property, missing belongings, or financial irregularities.

red_flag_categories.name: Behavioural Incident Requiring Reporting
red_flag_categories.description: Significant behavioural events including aggression, self-harm, absconding, or other behaviours requiring formal documentation.

red_flag_categories.name: Worker Safety / WHS Concerns
red_flag_categories.description: Threats to worker safety, unsafe work environment, or workplace health and safety breaches.

red_flag_categories.name: Environmental / WHS Hazards
red_flag_categories.description: Unsafe living conditions, environmental hazards, or significant WHS risks in the participant's home or community environment.

red_flag_categories.name: Participant Rights Violations
red_flag_categories.description: Actions that violate the participant's legal or human rights, including choice, control, dignity, and privacy.

red_flag_categories.name: Service Delivery Failures
red_flag_categories.description: Significant failures in service delivery, including missed shifts, lack of essential support, or failure to follow support plans.

red_flag_categories.name: Cultural Safety Concerns
red_flag_categories.description: Failures to provide culturally safe and appropriate support, particularly for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander participants or those from CALD backgrounds.

red_flag_categories.name: Pricing Fraud / Billing Irregularity
red_flag_categories.description: Indicators of overbilling, double-billing, claiming for undelivered services, or charging higher prices to NDIS participants.
keywords: claimed for cancelled shift without notice

required_forms.authority: Provider Internal Governance / NDIS Quality and Safeguards Commission

red_flag_categories.name: Unresolved Complaint / Grievance
red_flag_categories.description: Indicators of a participant or family member making a complaint that requires formal documentation and resolution.

required_forms.authority: Provider Internal Governance / NDIS Quality and Safeguards Commission
